creator: lars@chaotika.org
text:
Tried to change the syscall after intercepting a syscall via ptrace PT_TO_SCE to stop the process from executing that syscall using the following code:

http://alip.github.com/code/ptrace-freebsd-deny.c

The Expected behavior would be that the file foo.bar wouldn't be created as the syscall id was changed to something invalid(which (at least on Linux) works out of the box), but it seems like the original syscall was still executed.

Other approaches like calling PT_KILL instead of PT_GETREGS/PT_SETREGS didn't do it either.

I don't know if this is actually a Bug, but it is indeed a different behavior than on Linux, and it would be quite useful to have the same behavior on FreeBSD

for more Information see this mailing list thread:

http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-hackers/2010-January/030405.html

the attached patch is from that thread and should correct it on i386 and amd64

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
